source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By faith, Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By faith, Jacob when he was a dying blessed both the sons of Joseph, and worshiped leaning on the top of his staff.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By faith, Joseph when he died made mention of the departing of the children of Israel, and gave commandment concerning his bones.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Many prophets and righteous men” (it is said) “have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear and have not heard them.” ( Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Did then those righteous men know all the things to come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea, most certainly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if because of the weakness of those who were not able to receive Him, the Son was not revealed,—He was with good reason revealed to those conspicuous in virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This Paul also says, that they knew “the things to come,” that is the resurrection of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Or he does not mean this: but that “By faith, concerning things to come” [means] not [concerning] the world to come, but “concerning things to come” in this world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For how [except by faith] could a man sojourning in a strange land, give such blessings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But on the other hand he obtained the blessing, and yet did not receive it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that what I said with regard to Abraham, may be said also of Jacob, that they did not enjoy the blessing, but the blessings went to his posterity, while he himself obtained the “things to come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For we find that his brother rather enjoyed the blessing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For [Jacob] spent all his time in servitude and working as a hireling, and [amid] dangers, and plots, and deceits, and fears; and when he was asked by Pharaoh, he says, “Few and evil have my days been” ( Gen. xlvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 9 ); while the other lived in independence and great security, and afterwards was an object of terror to [Jacob].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Where then did the blessings come to their accomplishment, save in the [world] to come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou that from the beginning the wicked have enjoyed things here, but the righteous the contrary?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not however all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For behold, Abraham was a righteous man, and he enjoyed things here as well, though with affliction and trials.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For indeed wealth was all he had, seeing all else relating to him was full of affliction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is impossible that the righteous man should not be afflicted, though he be rich: for when he is willing to be overreached, to be wronged, to suffer all other things, he must be afflicted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that although he enjoy wealth, [yet is it] not without grief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why? you ask.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because he is in affliction and distress.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if at that time the righteous were in affliction, much more now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By Faith,” he says, “Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come” (and yet Esau was the elder; but he puts Jacob first for his excellence).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how great was his Faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whence did he promise to his sons so great blessings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Entirely from his having faith in God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By Faith, Jacob when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here we ought to set down the blessings entire, in order that both his faith and his prophesying may be made manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And worshiped leaning,” he says, “upon the top of his staff.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here, he means, he not only spoke, but was even so confident about the future things, as to show it also by his act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For inasmuch as another King was about to arise from Ephraim, therefore it is said, “And he bowed himself upon the top of his staff.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, even though he was now an old man, “he bowed himself” to Joseph, showing the obeisance of the whole people which was to be [directed] to him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this indeed had already taken place, when his brethren “bowed down” to him: but it was afterwards to come to pass through the ten tribes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how he foretold the things which were to be afterwards?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how great faith they had?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How they believed “concerning the things to come”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For some of the things here, the things present, are examples of patience only, and of enduring ill-treatment, and of receiving nothing good; for instance, what is mentioned in the case of Abraham, in the case of Abel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But others are [examples] of Faith, as in the case of Noah, that there is a God, that there is a recompense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (For Faith in this place is manifold, both of there being a recompense, and of awaiting it, not under the same conditions, and of wrestling before the prizes.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And the things also which concern Joseph are of Faith only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Joseph heard that [God] had made a promise to Abraham, that He had engaged His word “to thee and to thy seed will I give this land;” and though in a strange land, and not yet seeing the engagement fulfilled, but never faltered even so, but so believed as even to “speak of the Exodus, and to give commandment concerning his bones.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He then not only believed himself, but led on the rest also to Faith: that having the Exodus always in mind (for he would not have “given commandment concerning his bones,” unless he had been fully assured [of this]), they might look for their return [to Canaan].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore, when some men say, ‘See!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even righteous men had care about their sepulchers,’ let us reply to them, that it was for his reason: for he knew that “the earth is the Lord’s and all that therein is.” ( Ps. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He could not indeed have been ignorant of this, who lived in so great philosophy, who spent his whole life in Egypt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet if he had wished, it was possible for him to return, and not to mourn or vex himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But when he had taken up his father thither, why, did he enjoin them to carry up thence his own bones also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Evidently for this reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, are not the bones of Moses himself laid in a strange land?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And those of Aaron, of Daniel, of Jeremiah?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And as to those of the Apostles we do not know where those of most of them are laid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For of Peter indeed, and Paul, and John, and Thomas, the sepulchers are well known; but those of the rest, being so many, have nowhere become known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not therefore lament at all about this, nor be so little-minded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For whereever we may be buried, “the earth is the Lord’s and all that therein is.” ( Ps. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Certainly what must take place, does take place: to mourn however, and lament, and bewail the departed, arises from littleness of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 23 ) “By faith, Moses when he was born, was hid three months of his parents.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou see that in this case they hoped for things on the earth after their death?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And many things were fulfilled after their death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is for some who say, ‘After death those things were done for them, which they did not obtain while alive; nor did they believe [would be] after their death.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover Joseph did not say, He gave not the land to me in my life-time, nor to my father, nor to my grandfather, whose excellence too ought to have been reverenced; and will He vouchsafe to these wretched people what He did not vouchsafe to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said nothing of all this, but by Faith he both conquered and went beyond all these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He has named Abel, Noah, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, Joseph, all illustrious and admirable men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Again he makes the encouragement greater, by bringing down the matter to ordinary persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that the admirable should feel thus, is nothing wonderful, and to appear inferior to them, is not so dreadful: but to show oneself inferior even to people without names, this is the dreadful thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he begins with the parents of Moses, obscure persons, who had nothing so great as their son [had].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore also he goes on to increase the strangeness of what he says by enumerating even women that were harlots, and widows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “by Faith” (he says) “the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he mentions the rewards not only of belief but also of unbelief; as in [the case of] Noah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But at present we must speak of the parents of Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Pharaoh gave orders that all the male children should be destroyed, and none had escaped the danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whence did these expect to save their child?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What sort of Faith?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “They saw” (he says) “that he was a proper child.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The very sight drew them on to Faith: thus from the beginning, yea from the very swaddling-clothes, great was the Grace that was poured out on that righteous man, this being not the work of nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For observe, the child immediately on its birth appears fair and not disagreeable to the sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whose [work] was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not that of nature, but of the Grace of God, which also stirred up and strengthened that barbarian woman, the Egyptian, and took and drew her on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet in truth Faith had not a sufficient foundation in their case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what was it to believe from sight?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But you (he would say) believe from facts and have many pledges of Faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “the receiving with joyfulness the spoiling of their goods” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 34 ), and other such [things], were [evidences] of Faith and of Patience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But inasmuch as these [Hebrews] also had believed, and yet afterwards had become faint-hearted, he shows that the Faith of those [saints of old] also was long continued, as, for instance, that of Abraham, although the circumstances seemed to contend against it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And” (he says) “they were not afraid of the king’s commandment,” although that was in operation, but this [their hope respecting their child] was simply a kind of bare expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this indeed was [the act] of his parents; but Moses himself what did he contribute?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Next again an example appropriate to them, or rather greater than that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, saith he, ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 24–26 ) “by faith Moses when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh’s daughter, choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season; esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt; for he had respect unto the recompense of the reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As though he had said to them, ‘No one of you has left a palace, yea a splendid palace, nor such treasures; nor, when he might have been a king’s son, has he despised this, as Moses did.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that he did not simply leave [these things], he expressed by saying, “he refused,” that is, he hated, he turned away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when Heaven was set before him, it was superfluous to admire an Egyptian Palace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see how admirably Paul has put it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not say, ‘Esteeming heaven, and the things in heaven,’ ‘greater riches than the treasures of Egypt,’ but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The reproach of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the being reproached for the sake of Christ he accounted better than being thus at ease; and this itself by itself was reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Choosing rather” (he says) “to suffer affliction with the people of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For ye indeed suffer on your own account, but he “chose” [to suffer] for others; and voluntarily threw himself into so many dangers, when it was in his power both to live religiously, and to enjoy good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Than” (he says) “to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He called unwillingness “to suffer affliction with the” rest “sin”: this, he says, [Moses] accounted to be “sin.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then he accounted it “sin” not to be ready to “suffer affliction with” the rest, it follows that the suffering affliction must be a great good since he threw himself into it from the royal palace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But this he did, seeing some great things before him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is, “the reproach of Christ”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is being reproached in such ways as ye are, the reproach which Christ endured; Or that he endured for Christ’s sake: for “that rock was Christ” ( 1 Cor. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 4 ); the being reproached as you are.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But what is “the reproach of Christ”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That [because] we repudiate the [ways] of our fathers we are reproached; that we are evil-entreated when we have run to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It was likely that he also was reproached, when it was said to him, “Wilt thou kill me as thou killedst the Egyptian yesterday?” ( Ex. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is “the reproach of Christ,” to be ill-treated to the end, and to the last breath: as He Himself was reproached and heard, “If Thou be the Son of God” ( Matt. xxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 40 ), from those for whom He was crucified, from those who were of the same race.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is “the reproach of Christ” when a man is reproached by those of his own family, or by those whom he is benefiting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For [Moses] also suffered these things from the man who had been benefited [by him].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In these words he encouraged them, by showing that even Christ suffered these things, and Moses also, two illustrious persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that this is rather “the reproach of Christ” than of Moses inasmuch as He suffered these things from “His own.” ( John i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But neither did the one send forth lightnings, nor the Other feel any [anger], but He was reviled and endured all things, whilst they “wagged their heads.” ( Matt. xxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since therefore it was probable that they [the readers] also would hear such things, and would long for the Recompense, he says that even Christ and Moses had suffered the like.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So then ease is [the portion] of sin; but to be reproached, of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what then dost thou wish?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “The reproach of Christ,” or ease?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 27 ) “By faith he forsook Egypt not fearing the wrath of the king; for he endured as seeing Him who is Invisible.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What dost thou say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That he did not fear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet the Scripture says, that when he heard, he “was afraid” ( Ex. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 14 ), and for this cause provided for safety by flight, and stole away, and secretly withdrew himself; and afterwards he was exceedingly afraid.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Observe the expressions with care: he said, “not fearing the wrath of the king,” with reference to his even presenting himself again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it would have been [the part] of one who was afraid, not to undertake again his championship, nor to have any hand in the matter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That he did however again undertake it, was [the part] of one who committed all to God: for he did not say, ‘He is seeking me, and is busy [in the search], and I cannot bear again to engage in this matter.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that even flight was [an act of] faith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then did he not remain (you say)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That he might not cast himself into a foreseen danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this finally would have been tempting [God]: to leap into the midst of dangers, and say, ‘Let us see whether God will save me.’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this the devil said to Christ, “Cast Thyself down.” ( Matt. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou that it is a diabolical thing, to throw ourselves into danger without cause and for no purpose, and to try whether God will save us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he [Moses] could no longer be their champion when they who were receiving benefits were so ungrateful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It would therefore have been a foolish and senseless thing to remain there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But all these things were done, because, “he endured as seeing Him who is Invisible.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then we too always see God with our mind, if we always think in remembrance of Him, all things will appear endurable to us, all things tolerable; we shall bear them all easily, we shall be above them all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if a person seeing one whom he loves, or rather, remembering him is roused in spirit, and elevated in thought, and bears all things easily, while he delights in the remembrance; one who has in mind Him who has vouchsafed to love us in deed, and remembers Him, when will he either feel anything painful, or dread anything fearful or dangerous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When will he be of cowardly spirit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For all things appear to us difficult, because we do not have the remembrance of God as we ought; because we do not carry Him about alway in our thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For surely He might justly say to us, “Thou hast forgotten Me, I also will forget thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And so the evil becomes twofold, both that we forget Him and He us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For these two things are involved in each other, yet are two.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For great is the effect of God’s remembrance, and great also of His being remembered by us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The result of the one is that we choose good things; of the other that we accomplish them, and bring them to their end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore the prophet says, “I will remember Thee from the land of Jordan, and from the little hill of Hermon.” ( Ps. xlii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The people which were in Babylon say this: being there, I will remember Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore let us also, as being in Babylon, [do the same].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For although we are not sitting among warlike foes, yet we are among enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For some [of them] indeed were sitting as captives, but others did not even feel their captivity, as Daniel, as the three children (cf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ps. cxxxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 1 ); who even while they were in captivity became in that very country more glorious even than the king who had carried them captive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he who had taken them captive does obeisance to the captives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Dost thou see how great virtue is?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When they were in actual captivity he waited on them as masters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He therefore was the captive, rather than they.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It would not have been so marvelous if when they were in their native country, he had come and done them reverence in their own land, or if they had been rulers there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But the marvelous thing is, that after he had bound them, and taken them captive, and had them in his own country, he was not ashamed to do them reverence in the sight of all, and to “offer an oblation.” ( Dan. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do you see that the really splendid things are those which relate to God, whereas human things are a shadow?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He knew not, it seems, that he was leading away masters for himself, and that he cast into the furnace those whom he was about to worship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But to them, these things were as a dream.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us fear God, beloved, let us fear [Him]: even should we be in captivity, we are more glorious than all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let the fear of God be present with us, and nothing will be grievous, even though thou speak of poverty, or of disease, or of captivity, or of slavery, or of any other grievous thing: Nay even these very things will themselves work together for us the other way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: These men were captives, and the king worshiped them: Paul was a tent-maker, and they sacrificed to him as a God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here a question arises: Why, you ask, did the Apostles prevent the sacrifices, and rend their clothes, and divert them from their attempt, and say with earnest lamentation, “What are ye doing? we also are men of like passions with you” ( Acts xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 15 ); whereas Daniel did nothing of this kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that he also was humble, and referred [the] glory to God no less than they, is evident from many places.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Especially indeed is it evident, from the very fact of his being beloved by God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if he had appropriated to himself the honor belonging to God, He would not have suffered him to live, much less to be in honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Secondly, because even with great openness he said, “And as to me, O King, this secret hath not been revealed to me through any wisdom that is in me.” ( Dan. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again; he was in the den for God’s sake, and when the prophet brought him food, he saith, “For God hath remembered me.” ( Bel and the Dragon, ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus humble and contrite was he.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He was in the den for God’s sake, and yet he counted himself unworthy of His remembrance, and of being heard.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yet we though daring [to commit] innumerable pollutions, and being of all men most polluted, if we be not heard at our first prayer, draw back.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Truly, great is the distance between them and us, as great as between heaven and earth, or if there be any greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: After so many achievements, after the miracle which had been wrought in the den, dost thou account thyself so humble?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea, he says; for what things soever we have done, “we are unprofitable servants.” ( Luke xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus by anticipation did he fulfill the evangelical precept, and accounted himself nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “God hath remembered me,” he said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: His prayer again, of how great lowliness of mind it is full.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again the three children said thus, “We have sinned, we have committed iniquity.” ( Song of the Three Children, ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And everywhere they show their humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet Daniel had occasions innumerable for being puffed up; but he knew that these also came to him on account of his not being puffed up, and he did not destroy his treasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For among all men, and in the whole world he was celebrated, not only because the king cast himself on his face and offered sacrifice to him, and accounted him to be a God, who was himself honored as God in all parts of the world: for he ruled over the whole [earth]; (and this is evident from Jeremiah.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Who putteth on the earth,” saith he, “as a garment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (See Jer. xliii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 12 and Ps. civ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “I have given it to Nebuchadnezzar My servant” ( Jer. xxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 6 ), and again from what he [the King] says in his letter).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And because he was held in admiration not only in the place where he was, but everywhere, and was greater than if the rest of the nations had been present and seen him; when even by letters [the King] confessed his submission and the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But yet again for his wisdom he was also held in admiration, for it is said, “Art thou wiser than Daniel?” ( Ezek. xxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And after all these things he was thus humble, dying ten thousand times for the Lord’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then, you ask, being so humble did he not repel either the adoration which was paid him by the king, or the offerings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This I will not say, for it is sufficient for me simply to mention the question, and the rest I leave to you, that at least in this way I may stir up your thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (This however I conjure you, to choose all things for the fear of God, having such examples; and because in truth we shall obtain the things here also, if we sincerely lay hold on the things which are to come.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that he did not do this out of arrogance, is evident from his saying, “Thy gifts be to thyself.” ( Dan. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For besides this also again is another question, how while in words he rejected it, in deed he received the honor, and wore the chain [of gold]. ( Dan. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover while Herod on hearing the cry “It is the voice of a god and not of a man,” inasmuch as “he gave not God the glory, burst in sunder, and all his bowels gushed out” ( Acts xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 18 ), this man received to himself even the honor belonging to God, not words only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However it is necessary to say what this is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In that case [at Lystra] the men were falling into greater idolatry, but in this [of Daniel] not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For his being thus accounted of, was an honor to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore he said in anticipation, “And as to me, not through any wisdom that is in me.” ( Dan. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And besides he does not even appear to have accepted the offerings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he [the king] said (as it is written) that they should offer sacrifice, but it did not appear that the act followed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But there [at Lystra] they carried it even to sacrificing the bulls, and “they called” the one “Jupiter and” the other “Mercurius.” ( Acts xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The chain [of gold] then he accepted, that he might make himself known; the offering however why does it not appear that he rejected it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For in the other case too they did not do it, but they attempted it, and the Apostles hindered them; wherefore here also he ought at once to have rejected [the adoration].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And there it was the entire people: here the King.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why he did not divert him [Daniel] expressed by anticipation, [viz.] that [the king] was not making an offering [to him] as to a God, to the overthrow of religious worship, but for the greater wonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It was on God’s account that [Nebuchadnezzar] made the decree; wherefore [Daniel] did not mutilate the honor [offered].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But those others [at Lystra] did not act thus, but supposed them to be indeed gods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: On this account they were repelled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And here, after having done him reverence, he does these things: for he did not reverence him as a God, but as a wise man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But it is not clear that he made the offering: and even if he did make it, yet not that it was with Daniel’s acceptance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what [of this], that he called him “Belteshazzar, the name of” his own “god”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thus [it seems] they accounted their gods to be nothing wonderful, when he called even the captive thus; he who commands all men to worship the image, manifold and of various colors, and who adores the dragon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Moreover the Babylonians were much more foolish than those at Lystra.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore it was not possible at once to lead them on to this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And many [more] things one might say: but thus far these suffice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If therefore we wish to obtain all good things, let us seek the things of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as they who seek the things of this world fail both of them and of the others, so they who prefer the things of God, obtain both.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then not seek these but those, that we may attain also to the good things promised in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost, be glory, power, honor, now and for ever and world without end.
